name: Zipline
description: A traversal route where the player attaches to a cable or rail and moves along a predefined line between endpoints.
design_notes: Zipline traversal is constrained movement, so the player judges it by attach clarity, endpoint safety, detach control, and camera framing. It needs rules for direction, speed, collision, and what happens if the destination becomes unsafe.
design_purpose: Move players quickly along authored routes across gaps, arenas, or vertical spaces.
Create one-way or bidirectional traversal links that designers can reason about.
Offer a readable high-speed route with controlled risk and detach choices.
player_fantasy: The player feels carried along a fast route while choosing when to commit, ride, or detach.

parameters.name: zipline_attach_radius
parameters.description: Distance from handle or cable where attachment is allowed.

parameters.name: zipline_acceleration
parameters.description: Acceleration along the cable after attachment.

parameters.name: zipline_detach_impulse
parameters.description: Impulse applied when the player jumps or drops off.

parameters.name: endpoint_auto_detach_ms
parameters.description: Delay before automatic detach after reaching the end.

edge_cases: Endpoint landing space can become blocked by enemies, moving platforms, or other players.
Detaching mid-route should preserve route velocity in a predictable direction.
Multiple players on the same zipline need spacing, collision, or overlap rules.
Saving or streaming a level while attached must restore route progress and detach state safely.
common_bugs: The player remains parented to the zipline after detach and cannot move normally.
Cable progress overshoots the endpoint and reverses or jitters.
Camera faces the route direction but input still uses world movement axes.
Networked riders desync because clients simulate spline progress independently without correction.
balancing_notes: Fast ziplines compress travel time but can bypass encounter space unless endpoints are controlled.
Manual detach adds expression but can create unintended shortcuts if landing areas are not tested.
accessibility_notes: Use clear attach prompts and endpoint visibility so players know where the ride leads before committing.
Avoid strong camera banking on ziplines unless reduced-motion settings can disable it.

implementation_notes.unity: Represent the route as a spline or endpoints with progress t, attach the actor to route motion state, and detach by converting route velocity back to character velocity.
implementation_notes.godot: Use Path3D or explicit endpoints, update progress in _physics_process, and keep the player's collision shape active for endpoint clearance checks.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use spline components for the cable path and a custom movement mode or attach state that replicates route progress and detach events.
implementation_notes.web: Store zipline_id, route_t, and route_velocity on the actor, advance progress in the simulation, and validate endpoint clearance before auto-detach.

example_games.title: Apex Legends
example_games.note: Uses ziplines as fast traversal routes between positions.

example_games.title: Far Cry 3
example_games.note: Uses ziplines for quick downhill traversal and outpost movement.
